target: data/wins.json WIN-068 detail_verdict_text — delete P5 (the 'Even granting that FSF = H(r)/r is a valid geometric scaling…' paragraph) and replace with a one-sentence cross-reference to WIN-010's verdict. Closes curmudgeon WIN-068 c11 Hole 1 (ISS-1466).
patch_instructions: Single substring replacement in the WIN with id='068' in data/wins.json, field detail_verdict_text. Deletes P5 (the 'Even granting that FSF = H(r)/r is a valid geometric scaling…' paragraph, which attacks FSF via Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse) and replaces it with a one-sentence cross-reference to WIN-010's verdict, where FSF actually enters the predicted signal. Option (a) per the curmudgeon c11 recommendation. No other WIN-068 fields touched; no changes to WIN-010 needed (WIN-010 already carries the Coordinate Collapse critique in its own detail_verdict_text, verified this run).

patches.before: Even granting that FSF = H(r)/r is a valid geometric scaling, Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse) shows the disc-radius values that scaling feeds on are themselves computed from observed Polaris elevation angles, travelling through the same n(r) medium the dome concedes is 300–6,000× more refractive than Earth's atmosphere. Every dome-coordinate r in the FSF table inherits that contamination, so even the one genuine derivation the dome has produced anchors on numbers that are not what the dome thinks they are.
patches.after: For the Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse critique of the dome-coordinate r values that feed FSF, see WIN-010's verdict, where FSF actually enters the predicted signal and inherits that refractive contamination. It is out of scope here: the 39-eclipse ensemble evaluated in WIN-068 does not invoke FSF at all.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon WIN-068 c11 Hole 1 (moderate): detail_verdict_text P5 spends a full paragraph attacking FSF via the Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse, inside a WIN whose own scope sentences (detail_evidence P3 and detail_verdict_text P2) correctly say the 39-eclipse ensemble does not invoke FSF at all. This contradicts the scope discipline the c10 patch was explicitly designed to restore ('misattributed BOU clause removed, P3 scope declaration restructured') and gives a dome defender our own scope sentence to quote back at us. Option (a) is cleanest: delete P5 here and leave the Coordinate-Collapse critique in WIN-010's verdict where FSF actually enters the prediction. Verified 2026-04-23 that WIN-010 detail_verdict_text already carries the Coordinate Collapse paragraph ('Even granting that FSF = H(r)/r is a valid geometric scaling, Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse) shows the disc-radius values that scaling feeds on are themselves computed from observed Polaris elevation angles…') — so no WIN-010 edit is required, this is a clean scope fix.

holes_addressed.hole: WIN-068 c11 Hole 1 (moderate) — scope leak in detail_verdict_text P5
holes_addressed.description: detail_verdict_text P5 attacks FSF via Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse inside a WIN whose own scope sentences say FSF is a spectator for the 39-eclipse ensemble. A dome defender can quote our P2 scope sentence ('FSF does not enter the 1991–2016 data at all — it is only invoked for the 2026 forward projection') against our P5 FSF attack, showing we contradicted our own scope one paragraph after declaring it.
holes_addressed.fix: Delete P5 entirely and replace with a one-sentence cross-reference to WIN-010's verdict, where the Coordinate Collapse critique is on-scope (FSF actually enters WIN-010's predicted signal). The cross-reference preserves the inheritance relationship (readers following the cluster can still find the FSF critique) without re-opening the scope contradiction. The critique text itself already lives in WIN-010 detail_verdict_text, so no content is lost from the review.

new_evidence_added: No new evidence — content is being relocated, not added. WIN-010's verdict already carries the Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse paragraph (verified by direct wins.json read 2026-04-23), so the cross-reference in the replacement paragraph points to existing text.

anticipated_objections.objection: Deleting P5 removes a valid critique (the dome's r values are refraction-contaminated) from the WIN-068 verdict. Readers who land on WIN-068 first will miss it.
anticipated_objections.response: The critique is preserved in WIN-010's verdict, which is where FSF actually enters the predicted signal. Readers landing on WIN-068 first will see the cross-reference in the replacement paragraph and can follow it. The alternative (option b — keep P5 but add a scope lampshade) would leave a paragraph-long FSF attack inside a WIN whose own scope sentence says FSF is a spectator — a structural contradiction that rewards dome-defender quote-mining more than it rewards careful readers. Option (a) is the curmudgeon's recommended cleaner fix and preserves the content where it is on-scope.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not write option (b) — rewrite P5 with an explicit scope lampshade like 'We note this as inheritance-relevant to WIN-010 only — it does not affect WIN-068's ensemble verdict, which rests on Chapman Sq.'?
anticipated_objections.response: Option (b) was considered. It works structurally, but it costs more verdict real estate (the lampshade plus the attack) than a single cross-reference sentence, and it leaves a WIN-068 verdict paragraph whose entire purpose is to defer to WIN-010. A cross-reference sentence does the same work in one line without paragraph-length recapitulation. Option (a) also matches the cluster editing pattern already established by EXP-235 (WIN-025 detail_evidence) of keeping each WIN's critique scoped to what its own claim actually invokes.

anticipated_objections.objection: The cross-reference sentence is load-bearing for readers who skip around. If WIN-010's verdict later gets edited and the Coordinate Collapse paragraph moves, the cross-reference breaks silently.
anticipated_objections.response: The cross-reference uses the anchor '#win010' which is stable across verdict edits (the WIN anchor, not a paragraph ID). The Coordinate Collapse paragraph in WIN-010 is load-bearing for the FSF-retraction cluster argument (WIN-010/025/068) and is unlikely to be removed — any edit that removed it would also need to propagate the retraction to WIN-025 and WIN-068 as a cluster, which would be caught. If the paragraph is merely reworded, the cross-reference still lands on the right verdict. The fragility is low.

anticipated_objections.objection: Scope leaks are not worth a dedicated patch — this is rhetorical polish, not a factual error.
anticipated_objections.response: Scope discipline is a repeated curmudgeon finding in the WIN-010/025/068 cluster (c10 patches explicitly fixed scope; c11 found this residual leak). A dome defender who quotes our own scope sentence against our own subsequent attack lowers our credibility at exactly the pressure point where the cluster argument is strongest (FSF is real, retraction acknowledged, but imports magnitude from observation). Keeping scope tight at the cluster level is the rhetorical condition under which the substantive critique lands. The patch is small but the credibility effect is not polish.

kernel_of_truth: The deleted paragraph's content is correct and important — the dome's r coordinates are computed from observed Polaris elevation through a medium the dome itself concedes is 300–6,000× more refractive than Earth's atmosphere, so FSF = H(r)/r inherits that contamination. That critique belongs somewhere in the WIN-010/025/068 cluster. What this patch corrects is the location: P5 in WIN-068's verdict is the wrong home for the paragraph because WIN-068 does not invoke FSF at all (the 39-eclipse ensemble is a retrospective Kim & Chang citation). The paragraph's proper home is WIN-010's verdict, where FSF drives the 2026 forward projection and the coordinate contamination actually propagates into a counted prediction. WIN-010's detail_verdict_text already carries exactly this paragraph, so relocation here costs no content.
out_of_scope_deliberately: Curmudgeon WIN-068 c11 Hole 2 (minor) — P2 coverage-factor drop ('every other station's predicted delta_Z is a 2017 BOU number scaled by a geometric ratio'). Current wins.json text already reads 'scaled by the station's obscuration coverage and its FSF = H(r)/r geometric ratio' — the c11 review may predate a c10 patch that already fixed this at the 3-factor form. If a follow-up verification shows the text still drops coverage, that is a separate issue.

Curmudgeon WIN-068 c11 Hole 3 (minor) — P6 'invoke' subject ambiguity. Current wins.json text already reads 'all cite observations explained by the same ionospheric Sq mechanism' — appears already patched to the recommended wording.
Curmudgeon WIN-068 c11 Hole 4 (minor) — finding overreach on 'no dome-derived prediction'. Current finding already reads 'attaches no dome-derived numerical prediction' — appears already patched.
Curmudgeon WIN-068 c11 Hole 5 (minor) — Chapman 1933 over-attribution for eclipse-specific prediction. Separate issue, cluster-wide (applies also to WIN-010/WIN-025).
Curmudgeon WIN-068 c11 Hole 6 / ISS-1441 — Chapman 1933 citation format. Separate open issue.
Curmudgeon WIN-068 c11 Hole 7 / ISS-1442 — verbatim duplication of Tier-1 Steelman admission. Separate open issue.
Curmudgeon WIN-068 c11 Hole 8 / ISS-1443 — stale 'refraction fix' phrasing in detail_evidence P2. Separate open issue.

Curmudgeon's stronger_arguments suggestions (degrees-of-freedom asymmetry, magnetic-latitude mismatch, triple-count score deflation quantification). These are content additions for a future EXP, not the scope-leak fix queued here.
cross_win_consistency_flag: After integration, WIN-068 detail_verdict_text will no longer contradict its own scope (FSF is a spectator for the 39-eclipse ensemble, and the verdict will no longer spend a paragraph attacking FSF). The relocated critique lives in WIN-010 verdict where FSF actually enters the prediction — verified in-flight that WIN-010 detail_verdict_text already carries the Section 1.8 Coordinate Collapse paragraph. The WIN-010/025/068 cluster is cluster-consistent on the FSF retraction (all three concede FSF = H(r)/r as geometric) and now scope-consistent on the Coordinate Collapse critique (attached to WIN-010 where FSF propagates into a counted prediction, not to WIN-068 where FSF is dormant).
propagation_note: This EXP closes curmudgeon c11 Hole 1 only. Hole 5 (Chapman 1933 over-attribution) is the natural next cluster-wide pass because it applies to WIN-010/WIN-025/WIN-068 identically. ISS-1441/1442/1443 are separate pre-existing issues with independent fixes.
